next_step_text: 6/24/26 GK: IDB shared positive message, but based on internal agendas/team vacations the decision update will be early to mid-July 6/15/26 GK: check in with Mariana on IDB updates/expected decision which will be next week. 5/18/26 GK: PwC Digesh reference call with IDB 5/13 Reference call with PWC confirmed for Monday at 10am 5/11/26 GK: internal sync with Digesh from PwC 5/12, coordinating Diges

recent_activities_14d.subject: Add the known IDB buyer contacts as opportunity contact roles and multi-thread beyond a single contact.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of two finalists in a CLM procurement, confirmed 11 May 2026. The other finalist is unnamed and unknown — no competitive intelligence was gathered or logged during the 7.5-month cycle. Without knowing the competitor (likely SAP Ariba, Coupa, Icertis, or a regional CLM vendor), we cannot assess threat level, counter their positioning, or tailor the close strategy. The 44-day buyer silence and overdue close date suggest the other finalist may be winning or IDB has stalled the project. Threat level is marked high due to the lack of intelligence and the deal's current state (silent, overdue, no decision). Recommended move: re-engage IDB immediately, ask who the other finalist is, and learn how they are comparing the two solutions. Status is active because the bake-off is ongoing (no decision logged), but dormant is a risk if IDB has already chosen and not informed us.

competitors.name: Unknown finalist (one of two)
competitors.quote: IDB notified us as 1 of 2 finalists
competitors.how_we_win: Insufficient intelligence to position. Must surface the competitor identity, their pricing and fit gaps, and then anchor Merlin AI contract intelligence and iContract platform maturity as the wedge. Partner BCT must provide this intel or facilitate a direct buyer conversation.

competitors.name: Unknown finalist
competitors.quote: IDB notified us as 1 of 2 finalists
competitors.how_we_win: Identify the competitor immediately in the re-engagement call. If it is Ariba/Coupa/Ivalua, lean into Merlin AI negotiation agent as a differentiator and the integrated iContract+Merlin workflow. If it is a smaller or regional vendor, emphasize Zycus's enterprise scale, PwC reference (already delivered 18 May), and global support. Secure a direct conversation with the Economic Buyer to validate our commercial fit and push the decision.

champion_strength.summary: No confirmed internal IDB champion after 7-plus months. Mariana is the only named IDB-side touchpoint and her role/advocacy are unverified; PwC's Digesh is an external reference ally, not a champion. In a two-finalist race with no direct relationship, lack of a champion is the deal's biggest structural risk.

sources: Prefetched account contacts: Orlando Ferreira (CFO), Julio Cesar Lima (MD, IT&A)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged after roughly 7.5 months and a $100k deal. Account-level contacts include a CFO (Orlando Ferreira) and an IT Managing Director (Julio Cesar Lima), but neither is proven to be involved in this opportunity's scope and there are no opportunity contact roles on record (0 buyer-side contact roles). Budget authority and C-level sponsorship are unmapped, and because BCT runs all buyer access, Zycus cannot validate who signs. Mapping the EB through BCT, and ideally a direct introduction, is a ranked move before contracting.

recommended_moves: Press BCT to name the other finalist and IDB's decision criteria this week.
Have BCT surface IDB's specific MSA objections and target a resolution date.
Open one direct Zycus-to-IDB line by confirming Mariana's role and requesting an intro to the economic buyer.
